message:
🌱 Add vault: Add Redis Secret backend mission (#1001)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "vault-11697-add-redis-secret-backend",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "vault: Add Redis Secret backend",
+    "description": "Redis 6+ supports ACL-based users, making it possible to dynamically create and rotate credentials via Vault. This feature adds a Redis secret backend that allows operators to generate rotating Redis credentials instead of using hard-coded passwords. Requested by 39+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current Vault setup",
+        "description": "Verify your Vault version supports the Redis database secrets engine:\n```bash\nvault version\nvault secrets list\n```\nThe Redis database secrets engine requires Vault 1.12+ and Redis 6.0+ (which introduced ACL support)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Enable the database secrets engine",
+        "description": "Enable the database secrets engine if not already enabled:\n```bash\nvault secrets enable database\n```\nThis engine supports multiple database types including Redis."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure the Redis database connection",
+        "description": "Configure Vault to connect to your Redis instance:\n```bash\nvault write database/config/my-redis-db \\\n  plugin_name=redis-database-plugin \\\n  host=redis.example.com \\\n  port=6379 \\\n  tls=true \\\n  username=admin \\\n  password=admin-password \\\n  allowed_roles=\"my-redis-role\"\n```\nCreate a role that defines the credentials Vault will generate:\n```bash\nvault write database/roles/my-redis-role \\\n  db_name=my-redis-db \\\n  creation_statements='{\"commands\": [\"ACL SETUSER {{username}} on >{{password}} ~* +@all\"]}' \\\n  default_ttl=1h \\\n  max_ttl=24h\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify dynamic Redis credential generation",
+        "description": "Generate dynamic credentials and test them:\n```bash\n# Generate credentials:\nvault read database/creds/my-redis-role\n\n# Test the generated credentials against Redis:\n# The output will include a username and password that can be used\n# to authenticate to Redis with the ACL permissions defined in the role.\n```\nConfirm that credentials are generated with the correct TTL and that they work for authenticating to Redis."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Vault added a Redis database secrets engine that leverages Redis 6+ ACL support to dynamically create and rotate credentials. Operators can configure Vault with a Redis connection, define roles with ACL creation statements, and generate short-lived credentials that automatically expire, replacing hard-coded Redis passwords.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "vault write database/config/my-redis-db \\\n  plugin_name=redis-database-plugin \\\n  host=redis.example.com \\\n  port=6379 \\\n  tls=true \\\n  username=admin \\\n  password=admin-password \\\n  allowed_roles=\"my-redis-role\""
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "vault",
+      "community",
+      "secrets",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "vault"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/hashicorp/vault/issues/11697",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/hashicorp/vault",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/hashicorp/vault/pull/13541"
+    },
+    "reactions": 39,
+    "comments": 3,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "vault"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Vault server (1.12+) with admin access, and a Redis 6+ instance with ACL support enabled."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:49:58.156Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,

+  }
+}
